title: Add post-publish engagement seeding with self-reply threads and first-hour metric tracking
description: Add a post-publish engagement seeding phase that generates and posts 1-2 self-reply comments immediately after publishing to LinkedIn and Bluesky, and tracks first-hour engagement metrics to feed back into EIC topic scoring. LinkedIn's 360Brew algorithm (2026) weights first-hour engagement 2.3x for reach distribution, and only 5% of posts that fail in the first hour ever recover. Thoughtful comments (3+ sentences) are weighted 15x heavier than likes. Currently, MindPattern publishes and walks away — the critical first-hour window is unmanaged. This feature adds: (1) after successful post, generate 1-2 self-reply comments using voice.md that add genuine value (follow-up data points, related links from today's research, questions to spark discussion), (2) post self-replies with 5-15 minute jitter to appear organic, (3) send iMessage notification with post URL when published so user can engage in the first hour, (4) at T+60min and T+24h, fetch engagement metrics (likes, comments, reposts) via platform APIs and store in social_posts table, (5) feed first-hour metrics back to EIC as historical topic performance data. Distinct from R012 (posting time optimization) — this is about what happens AFTER publishing.
requirements: Add generate_self_replies() function to social/writers.py that takes the published post text + today's findings and generates 1-2 value-adding self-reply comments using voice.md
Add post_self_replies() to social/posting.py that replies to own post with 5-15 minute random jitter between replies
Modify social/pipeline.py post-publish flow to call generate_self_replies() and post_self_replies() after successful publication
Add iMessage notification via ApprovalGateway after post goes live with post URL and platform

Add engagement_metrics columns to social_posts table in memory.db (likes_1h INTEGER, comments_1h INTEGER, reposts_1h INTEGER, likes_24h INTEGER, comments_24h INTEGER, reposts_24h INTEGER, metrics_fetched_at TEXT)
Add fetch_post_metrics() to social/posting.py that retrieves current engagement counts via Bluesky AT Protocol getPostThread and LinkedIn v202504 socialActions endpoint
Add a lightweight metrics-fetch launchd job or hook that runs at T+60min and T+24h after posting to update engagement_metrics
Modify EIC prompt context to include last 7 days of topic → first-hour-engagement data so topic selection is informed by actual performance
done_criteria: After publishing, 1-2 self-reply comments appear on the post within 5-15 minutes
Self-replies add genuine value (follow-up data, questions) and pass the humanizer/policy checks
iMessage notification sent to user within 30 seconds of post going live
social_posts table shows first-hour metric columns populated for posts older than 1 hour
EIC prompt includes topic performance history from engagement metrics
All existing tests pass (python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q)
New tests verify self-reply generation produces valid content
New test verifies jitter timing is within 5-15 minute range
New test verifies engagement metrics are stored correctly

design_spec.architecture_impact: Extends the post-publish flow in social/pipeline.py. Adds self-reply generation to writers.py and reply-to-own-post to posting.py (Bluesky/LinkedIn clients). Adds columns to social_posts table. Requires either a delayed launchd job or an at-style scheduler for T+60min metric fetch. Modifies EIC prompt to include engagement history.

context: LinkedIn's 360Brew algorithm update (https://upgrowth.in/linkedin-algorithm-2026-360brew-update/) makes first-hour engagement the primary reach multiplier — 2.3x boost for posts with early engagement, and only 5% of posts that fail the first hour ever recover. Thoughtful comments (3+ sentences) are weighted 15x heavier than likes (https://predis.ai/resources/linkedin-algorithm/). Bluesky's chronological feed means early engagement determines initial visibility window. MindPattern publishes ~5 posts/week but does zero post-publish engagement management. Self-reply threads are a proven technique on both platforms for keeping posts in the feed and sparking discussion. Distinct from R012 (posting time optimization) which handles WHEN to post — this handles what happens AFTER.
